title: ws.rs nonce_is_fresh called on server-generated nonce (always passes)
description: In relay/src/ws.rs handle(), new_challenge() generates 'nonce' at the top of the function. After receiving the Authenticate response (within the 30-second timeout), the relay calls nonce_is_fresh(&nonce) on its own freshly-generated nonce (line 359). This check always passes because the nonce was created moments ago in the same function invocation. The intent (reject stale replayed challenges) is satisfied by per-connection nonce generation — each connection gets a unique nonce that cannot be reused across connections. But the nonce_is_fresh call is dead code and a misleading comment target. No security regression: auth is correct because the relay holds the nonce locally and the client cannot substitute a different one. Bug class: dead code that could mislead a future maintainer into thinking replay protection comes from this check.
